title: Mounting /sys fails with latest podman
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When running a container in which we mount /sys is now failing with latest podman (deployed from current master).

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Run a container which tries to mount /sys or something in /sys/*.

**Describe the results you received:**

```
"stdout: (cellv2) Creating default cell_v2 cell",
    "Error running ['podman', 'run', '--name', 'iscsid', '--label', 'config_id=tripleo_step3', '--label', 'container_name=iscsid', '--label', 'managed_by=paunch', '--label', 'config_data={\"start_order\": 2, \"healthcheck\": {\"test\": \"/openstack/healthcheck\"}, \"image\": \"docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-iscsid:0d5b1d18e88ad27e87368c8844c325cbfe603fdb_e25bec61\", \"environment\": [\"KOLLA_CONFIG_STRATEGY=COPY_ALWAYS\"], \"volumes\": [\"/etc/hosts:/etc/hosts:ro\", \"/etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro\", \"/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted:/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted:ro\", \"/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors:/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors:ro\", \"/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt:/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt:ro\", \"/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.trust.crt:/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.trust.crt:ro\", \"/etc/pki/tls/cert.pem:/etc/pki/tls/cert.pem:ro\", \"/dev/log:/dev/log\", \"/etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts:/etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts:ro\", \"/etc/puppet:/etc/puppet:ro\", \"/var/lib/kolla/config_files/iscsid.json:/var/lib/kolla/config_files/config.json:ro\", \"/dev/:/dev/\", \"/run/:/run/\", \"/sys:/sys\", \"/lib/modules:/lib/modules:ro\", \"/etc/iscsi:/var/lib/kolla/config_files/src-iscsid:ro\", \"/dev/null:/dev/null\"], \"net\": \"host\", \"privileged\": true, \"restart\": \"always\"}', '--detach=true', '--env=KOLLA_CONFIG_STRATEGY=COPY_ALWAYS', '--net=host', '--privileged=true', '--volume=/etc/hosts:/etc/hosts:ro', '--volume=/etc/localtime:/etc/localtime:ro', '--volume=/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted:/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted:ro', '--volume=/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors:/etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors:ro', '--volume=/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt:/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.crt:ro', '--volume=/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.trust.crt:/etc/pki/tls/certs/ca-bundle.trust.crt:ro', '--volume=/etc/pki/tls/cert.pem:/etc/pki/tls/cert.pem:ro', '--volume=/dev/log:/dev/log', '--volume=/etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts:/etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts:ro', '--volume=/etc/puppet:/etc/puppet:ro', '--volume=/var/lib/kolla/config_files/iscsid.json:/var/lib/kolla/config_files/config.json:ro', '--volume=/dev/:/dev/', '--volume=/run/:/run/', '--volume=/sys:/sys', '--volume=/lib/modules:/lib/modules:ro', '--volume=/etc/iscsi:/var/lib/kolla/config_files/src-iscsid:ro', '--volume=/dev/null:/dev/null', 'docker.io/tripleomaster/centos-binary-iscsid:0d5b1d18e88ad27e87368c8844c325cbfe603fdb_e25bec61']. [127]",
    "stderr: container create failed: container_linux.go:336: starting container process caused \"process_linux.go:399: container init caused \\\"rootfs_linux.go:58: mounting \\\\\\\"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/a38512ad1ebd3de216b5ff0be852084d4c192a0134de5efbc8fda1b1c424ae35/userdata/cgroup\\\\\\\" to rootfs \\\\\\\"/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/639053fb15c709d86cd2aafd357bbe3d68036fde6f9454765e0a9787f5058b8b/merged\\\\\\\" at \\\\\\\"/sys/fs/cgroup\\\\\\\" caused \\\\\\\"stat /sys/fs/cgroup/systemd/machine.slice/libpod-a38512ad1ebd3de216b5ff0be852084d4c192a0134de5efbc8fda1b1c424ae35.scope: no such file or directory\\\\\\\"\\\"\"",
    ": internal libpod error",
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container should start without error (like it did before).

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 0.9.2-dev
```

**Output of `podman info`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Running it on centos7 in a VM.

comments.body: Looking at this now. Probably a result of the recent patch to fix the `/dev/null` issue exposing another underlying `podman` issue (we would not have actually been mounting `/sys/` before, I believe)

I'm seeing a lot of SELinux issues with the `/sys/` mount over here - have to `setenforce 0` to get as far as you did. Definitely issues present other than SELinux, but we may want to look into adjusting the SELinux rules to make this work.

Further interesting news: This doesn't work on Docker 1.13. I'm testing here and getting angry CGroup errors.
I've added all volume mounts present, `--privileged`, and `--net=host`, and I cannot make this work on Docker with SELinux as Permissive
I wonder if you were launching the Docker daemon with any special configuration - maybe cgroupfs cgroups instead of systemd?

Trying cgroupfs got me further - now failing with `process_linux.go:279: applying cgroup configuration for process caused \"open /sys/fs/cgroup/cpuset/cpuset.cpus: no such file or directory\"` out of runc
Oh dear. I think whatever I was doing managed to completely wipe out `/sys/fs/cgroup` and that is why Docker was exploding.
Confirmed - Docker works with with your config when `/sys/fs/cgroup` is intact
Running the exact same command with `docker` replaced with `podman` wipes out /sys/fs/cgroup

Thanks @mheon for the investigation. Yes I run Docker on the host (with default config). Please let me know if you need more infos on my env. I can also give SSH with root access in this lab. Thanks again.

My initial thought was that this was an issue with spec generation, and we were generating the /sys/ bind mount or /sys/fs/cgroup in a sufficiently different way from Docker so as to cause problems. That does not appear to be the case. Moving on to other potential causes today.
Nevermind, talked with @rhatdan and we confirmed it was indeed volume mount permissions issues - we need to change all volume mounts from private to rprivate to match Docker and containerd

Changing all mount propagation from private to rprivate resolved the issue where the container was wiping out /sys/fs/cgroup, but we're still not able to launch the container. Will open a PR with the private -> rprivate change and continue debugging
Getting closer - I think it's the /dev/pts mount we're adding. It's missing from Docker. Maybe an artifact of the /dev/ mount?
I do recall this being an issue, and perhaps docker does not add /dev/pts if /dev/ is mounted over?

I've removed the /dev/pts mount, which moves us closer to the default Docker spec, but did not resolve the problem. Our current problem:
`conmon 3c2f02c7fb3d53954cf9 <ninfo>: console = {.name = '(null)'; .fd = 0}`
`conmon 3c2f02c7fb3d53954cf9 <error>: Failed to get console terminal settings Inappropriate ioctl for device`
I think this is resolved by our mount propogation changes
